summary: Operators from the "Rubikon" Center conducted FPV air defense operations against Ukrainian Armed Forces fixed-wing UAVs in the Donbas, Krasny Liman, and Sumy directions on March 6 and 7, 2026, destroying 107 and 88 targets respectively. In a separate development, Iran’s new subsonic loitering air defense missile, the Missile 358, equipped with a thermal imaging homing head, demonstrated effectiveness against Israeli Hermes-900 UAVs. Meanwhile, Ukrainian forces from Lasar’s Group, alongside SOF and National Guard units, successfully repelled mechanized assaults by the enemy in the Novopavlivka and Dobropillia directions, destroying enemy tanks. The "Rubikon" Center’s air defense units have intercepted over 1,500 fixed-wing UAVs, with more than 75% belonging to 11 specific models, including the Leleka-100, Furya, and others.
